summary: Iteration 10 content update verified end-to-end. All 11 writing posts render with correct slugs and testids. Gap Series posts show the 8-item full series index with proper 'Reading' active state on the current article; Point of View posts correctly omit the series index. ShareRow (LinkedIn/X/Email/Copy Link) renders with the expected testid prefixes on both writing details and Discernment. About page shows new KSU + personal-life copy and about-discernment-link. Discernment page at /about/discernment renders all required testids (title, back-to-about, save-pdf top/bottom, share row, email mailto, return/see-writing). Home hero, home-cta, footer-linkedin-link intact. Zero console errors across /, /work, /work/manufacturing-performance, /about, /about/discernment, /writing, /writing/the-gap-series-introduction, /writing/wrap-rage, /writing/cx-became-a-religion, /connect. Production build + strip-emergent.js pass with sanity check OK (id=root, main.js, main.css retained).

ui_bugs.issue: On Gap Series posts the chapter-num renders '01 / 07' (denominator uses gapSeries.length - 1 = 7, excluding the Introduction). The review spec explicitly expects '01 / 08'. Formula on line 44 is `${String(gapIndex).padStart(2, '0')} / ${String(gapSeries.length - 1).padStart(2, '0')}`. Should likely be `gapSeries.length` (=8) to match the '01 / 08' expectation and the 8-piece 'The full series' index shown just below.

integration_issues.issue: In the automated Chromium test the button label did not toggle to 'Copied' after click. Likely due to navigator.clipboard.writeText being unavailable/denied in the headless preview context; ShareRow silently swallows the error in a try/catch, so no visible state change occurs. In a real user browser (permission granted) this will work. Consider adding a document.execCommand('copy') textarea fallback so 'Copied' state fires even when clipboard API is blocked.

action_items: Fix chapter-num denominator in /app/frontend/src/pages/WritingDetail.jsx line 44 — change `gapSeries.length - 1` to `gapSeries.length` so it renders '01 / 08' as specified (and matches the 8-piece full-series index).
(Optional) Add execCommand fallback in ShareRow.copyLink so the 'Copied' state fires even when navigator.clipboard is denied.
critical_code_review_comments: ShareRow silently catches clipboard errors — no user feedback if copy fails. Consider a fallback + minimal error toast.

Discernment.jsx
inside the H1 makes automated text extraction return 'discernmentcomes' (no space). Visually correct, but if any SEO / a11y tool serialises the title it will read as one word. A single layout with responsive break would be cleaner.
context_for_next_testing_agent: All 11 writing slugs, ShareRow, Discernment, About discernment link, build+strip, and zero-console-error checks passed. Only two follow-ups: (1) chapter-num should render '01 / 08' not '01 / 07' — one-character code change; (2) Copy Link 'Copied' state won't display in headless test contexts without clipboard permission — verify manually in a real browser or add execCommand fallback.
rca of the issue: chapter-num bug: WritingDetail.jsx line 44 uses gapSeries.length - 1 (=7) as denominator. gapSeries includes the Introduction (gapIndex 0) plus 7 numbered posts = 8 items. Since Introduction gets its own label ('Introduction'), the -1 was likely intended to exclude the intro from the count. However, the review spec expects '01 / 08' (i.e., use the full length including intro). Fix: remove ' - 1' from the denominator string.
